title: Mildly Fermented French Lettuce Mille-Feuille
description: A satvik-friendly twist on the classic French pastry, featuring peppery lettuce and creamy ferment-kimchi. Perfect for a lazy Sunday morning or afternoon.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, sugar, and yeast.
Add the warm water to the dry ingredients and mix until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 10 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover with a damp cloth, and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour, until doubled in size.
Preheat the oven to 375° F (190° C).
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch.
Cut the dough into rectangles 4 inches wide and 6 inches long.
Bake the pastry for 15-20 minutes, until golden brown.
On a clean work surface, cut the lettuce leaves into thin strips.
In a small bowl, mix together the kimchi and confectioners' sugar.
Fill the pastry rectangles with a layer of lettuce, followed by a layer of kimchi mixture.
Repeat the layers one more time, finishing with a layer of pastry on top.
Bake for an additional 5-10 minutes, until the pastry is golden brown.
Let the pastry cool for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
